research.title: GitHub Code Scanning with CodeQL: Setup, Custom Queries, and Free Alternatives
research.whyTrending: Supply-chain attacks and shift-left security keep SAST top of mind; GitHub Advanced Security licensing changes in 2025-2026 pushed teams to evaluate CodeQL vs free alternatives like Semgrep and SonarQube

seo.suggestedTitle: GitHub Code Scanning with CodeQL: Setup Guide + Free Alternatives

gaps: Most CodeQL tutorials stop at default setup and never show the advanced workflow YAML in full
Almost nobody covers the licensing trap: CodeQL CLI is free ONLY for public repos, private repos need GitHub Code Security ($30/committer/month)
No article combines CodeQL setup with a realistic free-alternative stack (Semgrep CE, SonarQube CE, language-specific tools) with actual setup YAML
SARIF upload requirement for private repos (upload-sarif blocked without GHAS) is rarely explained
Custom query packs (qlpack.yml) treated as advanced black magic - no simple end-to-end example
strengths: Official GitHub docs are thorough on default vs advanced setup
Semgrep docs have good CI examples

expertInsights: CodeQL builds a queryable database from the AST + data flow of your code; queries are semantic, not regex
Default setup auto-picks languages/queries; advanced setup generates editable workflow YAML using github/codeql-action
Default setup analyzes JS/TS, Python, Ruby, Go automatically; compiled languages (Java/Kotlin, C/C++, C#, Swift) often need advanced setup with build steps
CodeQL CLI license: free for public/OSS repos and academic research only; closed-source analysis requires GitHub Code Security license
April 2025: GHAS split into GitHub Secret Protection ($19/committer/mo) and GitHub Code Security ($30/committer/mo), now also available on Team plan
SARIF upload to private repos requires GitHub Code Security enabled + permissions: security-events: write
Semgrep CE: LGPL-2.1, 30+ languages, 2000+ community rules, standalone mode needs no external service; semgrep-action deprecated - run CLI directly
Opengrep is the community fork of Semgrep created after the 2025 license change
Custom CodeQL query packs: qlpack.yml + dependency on codeql/<lang>-all; codeql pack init / pack install / pack publish to GHCR
Copilot Autofix can propose fixes for CodeQL alerts
commonProblems: Switching from default to advanced setup: must disable default first or SARIF uploads fail
upload-sarif 403 errors: missing security-events: write permission
Compiled languages failing in default setup due to missing build configuration
Surprise GHAS billing based on active committers
statistics: GitHub Code Security: $30/active committer/month; Secret Protection: $19
CodeQL supported: C/C++, C#, Go, Java/Kotlin, JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, Ruby, Swift

strategy.angle: Practical setup guide that leads with the licensing trap nobody mentions (CodeQL is only free for public repos) and delivers a complete free-alternative stack with working YAML for private repos
strategy.title: GitHub Code Scanning with CodeQL: Full Setup Guide (Plus Free Alternatives)
strategy.excerpt: CodeQL is one of the best SAST engines you can get, but the free part ends the moment your repo goes private. Here is how to set up CodeQL properly, write your first custom query, and build a completely free scanning stack with Semgrep and friends when the GHAS bill does not make sense.
hooks: Contrarian: the best code scanner on GitHub is free - until your repo is private, then it is $30 per committer per month
Concrete: default setup takes 4 clicks; the interesting part is everything after
Practical: a full free-stack workflow YAML you can paste today
outline: Hook: the pricing asterisk on 'free' code scanning
How CodeQL actually works (database, not regex)
Default setup: 4 clicks and what you get
Advanced setup: the workflow YAML explained line by line
Reading results: alerts, SARIF, severity, CWE
Custom queries: qlpack.yml and a real example
The licensing wall: what free actually means
Free alternatives: Semgrep CE, SonarQube CE, language-specific tools (Bandit/gosec/Brakeman), Trivy for the rest
A complete free scanning stack (workflow YAML)
Decision framework: when to pay for Code Security vs go free
